audit_status.source_base: Greek Acts text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for Hebrew defense, Damascus encounter retold, Ananias, temple vision, nations commission, crowd reaction, Roman citizenship.

verses.restored: Men, siblings and fathers, hear my defense now to you.
Hearing that he addressed them in the Hebrew dialect, they provided even more quiet. He says:

I am a Judean man, born in Tarsus of Cilicia but brought up in this city, educated at the feet of Gamaliel according to the strictness of the ancestral Law, being zealous for the Divine just as all of you are today.
I pursued this Way as far as death, binding and handing over to prisons both men and women,

as also the high priest and all the council of elders bear witness to me. From them I received letters to the siblings, and I journeyed to Damascus to bring also those there bound to Jerusalem to be punished.
It happened to me, as I was journeying and drawing near to Damascus about noon, suddenly from heaven a considerable light flashed around me.
I fell to the ground and heard a voice saying to me, Saul, Saul, why do you pursue me?
I answered, Who are you, Lord? He said to me, I am Jesus the Nazarene, whom you pursue.
Those with me beheld the light, but did not understand the voice of the one speaking to me.
I said, What shall I do, Lord? The Lord said to me, Rise and go into Damascus, and there it will be told to you concerning all things appointed for you to do.
Since I was not seeing from the glory of that light, being led by the hand by those with me, I came into Damascus.
A certain Ananias, a devout man according to the Law, well witnessed by all the Judeans dwelling there,
came to me, stood by me, and said to me, Brother Saul, see again. And in that very hour I looked up at him.
He said, The Divine of our fathers appointed you beforehand to know his will, to see the Righteous One, and to hear a voice from his mouth,
because you will be a witness for him to all humans of what you have seen and heard.
And now why do you delay? Rise, be baptized, and wash away your sins/misalignments, calling upon his name.
It happened when I returned to Jerusalem and was praying in the temple, that I came to be in a trance
and saw him saying to me, Hurry and go out quickly from Jerusalem, because they will not receive your witness concerning me.
I said, Lord, they themselves know that I was imprisoning and beating in every synagogue those trusting in you.
And when the blood of Stephen your witness was being poured out, I also stood by and approved, guarding the garments of those killing him.
He said to me, Go, because I will send you far away to the nations.
They were listening to him until this word, and they lifted up their voice, saying, Take such a one away from the earth, for it is not fitting for him to live.
As they were crying out, throwing off garments, and tossing dust into the air,
the commander commanded him to be brought into the barracks, saying that he should be examined by flogging, so that he might know for what cause they were shouting against him this way.
But when they stretched him forward with straps, Paul said to the centurion standing by, Is it lawful for you to flog a Roman citizen and uncondemned?
Hearing this, the centurion went to the commander and reported, saying, What are you about to do? For this human is Roman.
The commander came and said to him, Tell me, are you Roman? He said, Yes.
The commander answered, I acquired this citizenship for a large sum. Paul said, But I have been born so.
Immediately therefore those about to examine him withdrew from him, and the commander also was afraid, realizing that he was Roman and that he had bound him.

On the next day, wishing to know the certainty of why he was accused by the Judeans, he released him and commanded the chief priests and all the council to gather, and bringing Paul down, he stood him among them.
